mcqs.question: What is the output of: boolean result = (5 > 3) && (10 < 20);
mcqs.explanation: Both (5 > 3) is true and (10 < 20) is true, so true && true = true. && is short-circuit AND but both conditions are needed here.

mcqs.question: What is the output of: System.out.println(!true);
mcqs.explanation: The NOT operator (!) inverts the boolean value. !true equals false.

mcqs.question: What is the difference between & and &&?
mcqs.explanation: & (non-short-circuit) always evaluates both operands. && (short-circuit) stops evaluating if the first operand determines the result.

mcqs.question: What is the output of: boolean x = true; boolean y = false; System.out.println(x ^ y);
mcqs.explanation: XOR (^) returns true when exactly one operand is true. true ^ false = true.

mcqs.question: What is the output of: boolean result = (2 == 3) || (5 < 10);
mcqs.explanation: (2 == 3) is false but (5 < 10) is true. false || true = true using short-circuit OR.

mcqs.question: Which operator has the highest precedence among these: ||, &&, !?
mcqs.explanation: In Java, NOT (!) has highest precedence among boolean operators, followed by AND (&&), then OR (||).

mcqs.question: What is De Morgan's Law: !(A && B) equals?
mcqs.explanation: De Morgan's Law states that NOT of AND equals OR of NOTs: !(A && B) = !A || !B

mcqs.question: What will happen if you use short-circuit operators with side effects?

options.text: Both side effects will always execute

mcqs.explanation: With && and ||, if the first operand determines the result, the second operand is not evaluated, so any side effects (like method calls or increments) in the second expression are skipped.

mcqs.question: What is the default value of a boolean array in Java?
mcqs.explanation: The default value for boolean array elements is false, same as other primitive types.

mcqs.question: What is the output: boolean a = false; int b = 5; if(a & (b++ > 0)) {} System.out.println(b);
mcqs.explanation: & evaluates both operands. Since a is false, the condition is false but b++ still executes (post-increment), so b becomes 6.

mcqs.question: What is the output: boolean a = false; int b = 5; if(a && (b++ > 0)) {} System.out.println(b);
mcqs.explanation: && uses short-circuit. Since a is false, the second operand is not evaluated, so b++ doesn't execute and b remains 5.

mcqs.question: Which of these is NOT a boolean logical operator in Java?
mcqs.explanation: Java boolean logical operators are: & (AND), | (OR), ^ (XOR), ! (NOT), && (short-circuit AND), || (short-circuit OR). # is not an operator.

mcqs.question: What is the result of true | false?
mcqs.explanation: OR returns true if at least one operand is true. true | false = true.

mcqs.question: What is the result of false && false?
mcqs.explanation: AND returns true only when both operands are true. false && false = false.

mcqs.question: What does Boolean.TRUE represent in Java?

options.text: A Boolean object set to true

mcqs.correctAnswer: A Boolean object set to true
mcqs.explanation: Boolean.TRUE is a constant Boolean object (wrapper class) that represents the true value as an object, useful in contexts requiring objects.

mcqs.question: What is the truth table for XOR (^)?
mcqs.explanation: XOR returns true only when operands are different. true^false = true, true^true = false, false^false = false.

mcqs.question: Which operator is used for logical NOT in Java?
mcqs.explanation: The logical NOT operator in Java is the exclamation mark (!). It inverts boolean values.

mcqs.question: What is the output of: System.out.println(!(5 > 3));
mcqs.explanation: (5 > 3) is true, so !(true) is false.

mcqs.question: Which method converts String to boolean primitive?
mcqs.explanation: Boolean.parseBoolean(String s) returns the boolean primitive represented by the string. It returns false for any string other than "true" (case-insensitive).

mcqs.question: What is the result of De Morgan's Law: !(A || B)?
mcqs.explanation: De Morgan's Law: NOT of OR equals AND of NOTs. !(A || B) = !A && !B
